name: CENTRAL GARDEN & PET CO
business: Central Garden & Pet is a market leader in the U.S. pet and garden industries, selling branded and private label products for pet supplies (dog/cat, aquatics, small animal, equine) and lawn & garden consumables (grass seed, wild bird feed, controls, fertilizers, live plants). Products are sold to retailers including home centers, mass merchants, pet specialty stores, and eCommerce channels.

exposures.sentence: We are a leader in both the U.S. pet supplies market and the lawn and garden consumables market.
We continue to expand our digital and eCommerce capabilities while ensuring the right policies, products and programs enable all channels to compete effectively.

Our pet sales and distribution network supports almost 10,000 retailer locations and over 5,600 veterinary offices, with services ranging from shelf placement and replenishment, to tailored retailer programs, product launches and exposure for acquired brands.

We also provide supplies for aquatics, small animals, reptiles and pet birds, such as toys, enclosures, habitats, bedding, food and supplements, equine and livestock products, animal and household health solutions and insect control items.

Our Garden segment includes lawn and garden consumables such as grass seed; vegetable, flower and herb packet seed; wild bird feed, bird houses and other birding accessories; weed, grass, and other herbicides, insecticide and pesticide products; fertilizers and live plants.

catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, we decided to wind-down our operations in the United Kingdom, which also served certain European markets, and move to a direct-export model.

catalysts.event: Consolidation of two legacy distribution facilities into a new facility in Salt Lake City
catalysts.sentence: During fiscal 2025, we began the consolidation of two legacy distribution facilities in Ontario, California and Salt Lake City, Utah into a new modern facility in Salt Lake City, Utah, reflecting our ongoing network optimization initiative to achieve a simpler, more efficient distribution network.

catalysts.event: Authorized $100 million increase in share repurchase program
catalysts.sentence: In December 2024, our Board of Directors authorized a $100 million increase in the share repurchase program (the "2024 Repurchase Authorization").

catalysts.event: Entered into Fourth Amended and Restated Credit Agreement
catalysts.sentence: On November 7, 2025, we entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Credit Agreement (the Credit Agreement).

tamClaims.claim: In 2025, U.S. retail sales of pet food, treats and chews, supplies, veterinary and non-medical services and live animals will total approximately $158 billion, with about $40 billion attributable to the categories where we compete.
tamClaims.sentence: Packaged Facts estimated that in 2025, U.S. retail sales of pet food, treats and chews, supplies, veterinary and non-medical services and live animals will total approximately $158 billion, with about $40 billion attributable to the categories where we compete.

tamClaims.claim: The total lawn and garden consumables industry in the United States is estimated to be approximately $33 billion in annual retail sales in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: The total lawn and garden consumables industry in the United States is estimated by Packaged Facts, the Freedonia Group, Numerator and internal estimates to be approximately $33 billion in annual retail sales in 2025, including grass and other seeds, fertilizer, controls, live goods, wild bird products as well as soil and mulch.

tamClaims.claim: We estimate the annual retail sales of the lawn and garden consumables market in the categories in which we participate to be approximately $32 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the annual retail sales of the lawn and garden consumables market in the categories in which we participate to be approximately $32 billion.
